name: Ziggy Elman
abstract: Harry Aaron Finkelman (May 26, 1914 – June 26, 1968), known professionally as Ziggy Elman, was an American jazz trumpeter associated with Benny Goodman, though he also led his own group, Ziggy Elman and His Orchestra.

concerts.label: Benny Goodman and His Swing Orchestra
Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
Midnight Performance: Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Ziggy Elman, I think, was Jewish background. But the thing was as solid as a family, the Goodman thing. We were all there, just like brothers.

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: He had people in there like Charlie Shavers the great trumpet player, Ziggy Elman was still in the band then...
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: Benny looks at me and he says, 'I asked you to get me Ziggy Elman on the trumpet' he says. 'That’s Ziggy Elman, I flew him in' and Joe Glaser would use every F word in the world, you know I’m telling you, if I told him, he said, 'That’s not Ziggy Elman.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: listening to the rehearsals and having marvelous experiences like Gene Krupa or Harry James or Ziggy Elman saying, “Hey, kid, get me some coffee and tell the cook to make me a ham sandwich.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Ziggy Elman [Harry Finkelman] was a damn good choice there too and Bunny before it, Bunny Berigan, those were trumpet players, they were very heroic trumpet players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jean Bach
about_quotes.evidence: Well I wasn’t there, it was before I came into Bob’s life... And Ziggy Elman got up at bat and he had this big cigar in the way, it discombobulated the pitcher.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jean Bach interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995
